title: Chinese startup Spark Space tests engine, raises funds for electric-pump rocket
summary: Chinese startup Spark Space has conducted engine tests and secured funding rounds for its planned electric-pump-fed rocket, which it claims will be the world's largest. The company aims to develop a groundbreaking rocket using this innovative propulsion technology.

title: A legacy to help solve the space workforce pipeline
summary: The Challenger Center's president and CEO, Mike Kincaid, discusses the organization's role in addressing the space workforce pipeline in an interview with Space Minds. The discussion highlights the center's efforts to inspire and train the next generation of space professionals.

title: What the satellite servicing economy can borrow from carbon credits
summary: The FCC authorized 15,000 Starlink Gen2 satellites in January 2026, while companies like Starcloud and SpaceX have filed for massive orbital satellite deployments, totaling 88,000 and 1 million, respectively. The article discusses how the satellite servicing economy could adopt principles from carbon credits to manage orbital sustainability.

title: Boeing demonstrates quantum protocol in payload set for 2027 launch
summary: Boeing successfully demonstrated a quantum networking protocol in ground testing for a payload scheduled for a 2027 launch. The experiment involved high-fidelity entanglement swapping, a key step for future quantum communications in space.

title: Swift reboost mission ready for launch
summary: A high-risk mission to raise the orbit of NASA's Swift astrophysics spacecraft is set to launch later this month after less than a year of development. The mission aims to extend the spacecraft's operational lifespan.

title: Blue Origin begins rebuilding New Glenn pad
summary: Blue Origin has initiated the rebuilding of a launch pad that was severely damaged in a New Glenn rocket explosion just under three weeks ago. The company aims to resume launches by the end of the year.

title: Quantum Space wins Pentagon contract to develop orbital refueling spacecraft
summary: Quantum Space has secured a Pentagon contract to develop an orbital refueling spacecraft, aiming to deliver the fuel-transfer vehicle to the U.S. Space Force by 2028.

title: Relativity Space to privately develop Mars orbiter mission
summary: Relativity Space announced plans to privately develop and launch a Mars orbiter mission in 2028. This initiative marks the company's expansion into planetary exploration beyond Earth.

title: Upgraded Ariane 6 launches Amazon Leo satellites
summary: An upgraded Ariane 6 rocket successfully launched 36 Amazon Leo satellites on June 17. The European Space Agency (ESA) is considering options to increase the vehicle's launch rate.

title: America’s next economic frontier is 240,000 miles away
summary: America's next economic frontier is seen as the Moon, 240,000 miles away, with a focus on its potential for future economic opportunities. The article highlights the historical significance of the Apollo missions and the renewed interest in lunar exploration.

title: These Mars valleys are hiding 30 dust devils | Space photo of the day for June 18, 2026
summary: Europe's Mars Express orbiter captured an image of a vast valley system on Mars, revealing the presence of 30 dust devils across the landscape.

title: 'It's quite a bit more than we expected': Satellite reveals immense scale of GPS signal tampering
summary: An experimental satellite has mapped the scale of GPS jamming across Europe and the Middle East from space. The findings reveal a much larger issue than previously anticipated.

title: NASA's Perseverance rover just ran a marathon on Mars. Could you do the same?
summary: NASA's Perseverance rover has traveled over 26.2 miles on Mars, completing a marathon distance. This marks a significant milestone in the rover's mission on the Red Planet.

title: One of these twin stars has likely been snacking on exoplanets
summary: Astronomers found chemical differences between twin stars, suggesting one has consumed at least one planet. This discovery highlights the dynamic and sometimes destructive interactions in planetary systems.

title: James Webb Space Telescope discovers extreme exoplanet being roasted by its home star
summary: Astronomers using the James Webb Space Telescope discovered an extreme exoplanet being intensely heated by its host star, classifying it as a 'Hot Jupiter'. The findings highlight the extreme conditions of such planets orbiting close to their stars.

title: Warframe's latest update is all about space combat, constellations, and cosmic mythology
summary: Warframe's latest update introduces space combat, constellations, and cosmic mythology, blending old mechanics with new ideas. Digital Extremes expands the game's universe with a focus on celestial themes.

title: A volcanic eruption seen from space | Space photo of the day for June 17, 2026
summary: A volcanic eruption on Hawaii's Kīlauea volcano was captured from space, marking its 49th known lava outburst. The event was highlighted in a space photo of the day for June 17, 2026.

title: Hubble Glimpses Merging Galaxy Clusters
summary: The NASA Hubble Space Telescope captured an image of a merging galaxy cluster, CL0016+1609 or MACS J0018.5+1626, which is bright at X-ray wavelengths and has been extensively studied. Researchers observed that the cluster is actually two merging clusters along the line of sight.

title: El Niño Is Underway
summary: Satellite observations confirm the strengthening of the 2026 El Niño event in early June. This follows the onset of El Niño conditions earlier in the year.

title: NASA Announces Public-Private Partnership to Advance Mars Science
summary: NASA announced a public-private partnership to advance Mars science, with NASA providing the Aeolus instrument suite and Relativity Space handling the spacecraft, rocket, and cruise operations. This collaboration highlights NASA's increasing reliance on commercial innovation for deep-space missions.

title: Search for Hidden Cosmic Companions in Sun’s Backyard
summary: Astronomers are searching for hidden cosmic companions, such as brown dwarfs, orbiting stars to determine their ages. These brown dwarfs, which are too massive to be planets but too small to be stars, provide valuable insights into stellar evolution.

title: NASA’s Fermi Mission Uncovers Possible Sibling Supernova Remnants
summary: NASA's Fermi Mission discovered two supernova remnants that likely originated from a pair of once-orbiting stars. The first star's explosion ejected its companion, which later also detonated after traveling thousands of years.

title: Hubble Sees Swarm of Galaxies
summary: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope captured an image of the galaxy cluster MACS0329-0211, resembling a swarm of bees. Galaxy clusters like this are crucial for understanding the universe's structure and evolution.

title: NASA’s Webb Catches Exoplanet Getting Roasted
summary: NASA's James Webb Space Telescope observed an exoplanet, HD 80606 b, getting heated as it passes close to its star. The exoplanet has an extreme elliptical orbit and is four times the mass of Jupiter.

title: Astronaut Jessica Meir Assists With Hardware Updates for NASA’s Cold Atom Lab
summary: NASA astronaut Jessica Meir installed hardware updates for the Cold Atom Lab (CAL) aboard the International Space Station. CAL cools atoms to near absolute zero to study quantum phenomena.

title: NASA Webb, Hubble Reveal History of Relic of Milky Way’s Formation
summary: Researchers using NASA's James Webb and Hubble Space Telescopes discovered that Terzan 5 is not a globular star cluster, providing new insights into the formation and evolution of galaxies like the Milky Way.

title: Terran R second stage heads to Stennis as Relativity advances toward 2026 debut
summary: Relativity Space is advancing toward the 2026 debut of its Terran R heavy-lift rocket, with the second stage now headed to Stennis Space Center for testing. This marks a key milestone in the development of the fully reusable launch vehicle.

title: Launch Preview: 10 launches scheduled from sites across the world
summary: Ten launches are scheduled across multiple global sites in the upcoming week, marking a busy period for space industry activities.

title: Isar Aerospace scrubs second launch of Spectrum rocket after weeks of delays
summary: Isar Aerospace has scrubbed the second launch attempt of its Spectrum rocket after multiple delays. The European private space launch provider is based in Ottobrunn, Germany.

title: Booster 20 Completes Cryo, As Ship 40 Nears Static Fire
summary: Booster 20 has completed cryogenic testing, while Ship 40 is nearing a static fire test. Flight 12 was completed about three weeks prior.

title: China successfully debuts tallest rocket, LandSpace prepares for second landing attempt
summary: China successfully launched its tallest rocket, while LandSpace is preparing for a second attempt at a rocket landing. The month is expected to feature multiple missions for a single internet constellation.

title: Japan’s H3 to returns to flight with debut launch of lightest configuration
summary: Japan's H3 rocket successfully returned to flight with its debut launch of the lightest configuration. This marks a significant milestone for the country's space program after previous setbacks.

title: Stoke Space completes Nova Stage 1 structural verification
summary: Stoke Space has successfully completed the proto-qualification structural verification of the first stage of its Nova rocket. This milestone marks a significant step in the development of the company's reusable launch vehicle.

title: Artemis III Crew Named: Veteran Astronauts to test Lunar Landers in LEO
summary: NASA has named the four astronauts who will fly the Artemis III mission to test lunar landers in low Earth orbit (LEO). This crewed mission marks a significant step toward returning humans to the Moon.

title: Booster 20 into Cryogenic Testing ahead of Starship Flight 13
summary: SpaceX's Starbase teams are preparing Booster 20 for cryogenic testing ahead of the upcoming Starship Flight 13. This follows the recent conclusion of Starship Flight 12.

title: Launch Preview: Chinese, Rocket Lab, and Starlink flights scheduled
summary: The upcoming week will see launches from China, Rocket Lab, and Starlink, including three Starlink missions. Flights are scheduled from California, Virginia, and Florida.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary by releasing a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over the past five decades. The publication underscores the agency's significant contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: One robotic arm to rule them all
summary: The article discusses a new robotic arm designed for space applications. It highlights the potential of this technology to revolutionize space operations.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and ASPIICS instrument are fully operational, resuming formation flying to create artificial eclipses for scientific observations.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports research institutes in participating in European space science activities and missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online attendance option.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: ESA's Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section tests materials for spacecraft to ensure they survive space conditions. The section is supported by young researchers contributing innovative ideas.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) for training, operations, simulations, and public engagement in space exploration. This initiative aims to enhance efficiency and accessibility in space activities.

title: Sensitive and sturdy
summary: The news headline 'Sensitive and sturdy' suggests a focus on robust or resilient technology, likely in the space industry. However, the provided text lacks specific details about the event or organization.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: The article discusses a critical test for a spacecraft parachute system, referred to as 'the great parachute bake-out.' This test ensures the parachute's functionality under extreme conditions.

title: Boeing Completes Quantum Lab Test Ahead of 2027 Flight
summary: Boeing successfully demonstrated quantum entanglement swapping in ground tests for its Q4S quantum networking satellite. The company plans to launch the satellite in 2027.

title: True Anomaly Declares Mission X-3 Success
summary: True Anomaly announced the successful completion of Mission X-3 with its autonomous orbital vehicle, Jackal, meeting all test objectives and being fully commissioned. The mission marks a significant milestone for the company's orbital operations.

title: Instinct Space Unveils Plans for Low-Cost Lunar Landers
summary: Instinct Space is shifting its focus from navigation support for lunar missions to developing low-cost lunar landers. The company aims to directly contribute to lunar surface missions.

title: Katalyst Raises $12M to Extend Satellite Servicing to GEO
summary: Katalyst Space Technologies has raised $12 million to expand its satellite servicing capabilities to geostationary orbit (GEO). The company plans to launch a mission closer to Earth this month, followed by its first GEO mission in 2027.

title: Dawn Aerospace Raises $25M Series B
summary: Dawn Aerospace secures $25M in Series B funding to accelerate the development of its Aurora spaceplane and conduct an in-space refueling demonstration.

title: Bill Spotlight: NEW HORIZON Act
summary: A bipartisan bill called the NEW HORIZON Act has been introduced to allow national security missions to utilize commercial orbital data centers. The legislation aims to leverage the growing trend of commercial space-based data infrastructure.

title: Optera Raises £3M to Open New UK HQ
summary: Optera secured £3 million in funding to establish a new UK headquarters, aligning with the UK government's push to enhance domestic technical capabilities.

title: PiLogic Partners with AFRL on AI Anomaly Detection Tech
summary: PiLogic has partnered with AFRL to develop AI anomaly detection technology, currently testing it on terrestrial flat sats with plans to expand into orbit.

title: Space Stocks Dip After SpaceX IPO
summary: SpaceX's successful IPO led to a pullback in other publicly traded space companies, causing major players to decline. The dip highlights investor caution despite SpaceX's strong market debut.

title: JAXA Nails H3 Return to Flight
summary: JAXA successfully launched the H3 rocket on its eighth mission, marking its first successful flight with a new three-engine variant. The rocket delivered all six payloads to space from Japan's Tanegashima Space Center.

title: Intellian Calls New Production Facility in California a ‘Defining Milestone’
summary: Intellian Technologies is opening a new large-scale manufacturing facility in California, marking a significant milestone for the company. The facility is described as a defining achievement by CEO Eric Sung.

title: Lyntris Acquires Robotics Firm Novium to Further Satellite Offerings
summary: Lyntris, a recently formed defense tech company, acquired Houston-based robotics firm Novium to expand its satellite offerings. The acquisition follows Lyntris's formation through the merger of Vitesse.

title: Bezos Earth Fund Invests in FireSat Constellation Build-Out
summary: The Bezos Earth Fund is investing $26 million to support the development of the FireSat constellation, a wildfire-monitoring satellite system. This initiative aims to enhance early wildfire detection and response capabilities.

title: Thursday Morning Conversation With Tina Ghataore
summary: The article features an interview with Tina Ghataore, a former Satellite Executive of the Year, discussing future developments in the satellite industry. It also includes breaking news related to the space sector.

title: Gitai Delays Robotic Servicing Mission to Prioritize SBI Work
summary: Gitai has completed a flight model for a robotic on-orbit servicing mission but is delaying the mission to prioritize space-based interceptor (SBI) technologies following a recent U.S. Space policy update.

title: DHS Promotes Latest Space Cybersecurity Research in SPARTA
summary: The Department of Homeland Security's Science and Technology Directorate has unveiled new space cybersecurity research under the SPARTA framework to safeguard critical infrastructure. The initiative aims to enhance protection against cyber threats in the space sector.

title: The Space Sector Under Cyber Fire in the US-Israel-Iran War
summary: The US and Israel launched Operation Epic Fury, involving airstrikes and cyber/electronic operations against Iran, amid heightened tensions. The space sector is reportedly under cyber threats as part of this conflict.

title: SpaceX Launches 3 AST SpaceMobile BlueBirds in Stacked Mission
summary: SpaceX successfully launched three AST SpaceMobile BlueBird satellites in a stacked mission, marking the first time three satellites were deployed in a single launch fairing. The mission included BlueBird 8, 9, and 10 satellites.

title: Eutelsat Secures First Order Under French Defense Agreement
summary: Eutelsat has secured its first order under a new agreement with the French Ministry of the Armed Forces and Veterans. The company also announced additional deals, including one for Low-Earth Orbit capabilities.

title: All.Space Relocates to US Amid York Space Acquisition Process
summary: All.Space has moved its operations from the U.K. to Alabama, USA, aligning with U.S. defense priorities during its acquisition by York Space Systems.
